notes: Likely real fix for the Android MSC investigation: MSC mode's raw SD driver was running the card at 4MHz SPI clock (sdcard_init + MSC_SPI_SETTINGS, GenRuntime.ino), 5x slower than normal boot's proven-reliable 20MHz SD.begin(). Confirmed via 1.10.31's BLE-mirrored failure counters that reads/writes were succeeding, not failing, even during the exact 'loading files' action that triggered Android's warning -- pointing at sustained-transfer latency against Android's tighter SCSI timeout tolerance (Windows just has more patience), not data corruption or a card-compatibility bug. Bumped both to 20MHz to match normal boot. Includes all of 1.10.28-1.10.31's fixes.
